author: @NuclearMex
text: I cannot seem to find much literature of the mechanical alphabet, and what type of mechanisms are shown.

author: @DIY_Dad_Pat
text: Cracking idea of a mechanical alphabet would be helpful for teaching

author: @jimsmindonline
text:
For me I've enjoyed this series. Mechanical engineering like this is something I know very little about, never studied, yet is everywhere. 👍
Plus this is the fun sort of project you could do with minimal tools and get a cool looking object at the end.

author: @mwm2929
text: Quite inspiring for me as I build prototypes for people!  I am building the blocks to fit together with a standard set of 4 bolt holes on each side.   All my power supply components are on a short piece of fence board with input and output spade terminals.  I was thinking about speed of construction for a functional system with reusable parts already, but this mechanical alphabet should really help me in the long run!

author: @hissst69
text: Great idea :) as an aside, Why didn't you go for the magnetic bearing with reverse configuration? for a frictionless whimhurse. maybe because it wouldn't look of the period?

author: @3dmaker699
text: Great idea Robert.

author: @michaelschauperl172
text: Maybe its catastrophic thinking but ? Your're as well preparing people, should there ever be EMP Strikes on a country. And man you are inspiering people to learn. Well done!

author: @darrellpidgeon6440
text: That peg-gear module is a beautiful piece of work!

author: @curtisnixon5313
text:
FYI Robert, I had never heard of the mechanical alphabet before your videos. Are you aware that there are no other videos on utub about this, and precious few references come up on google search either? All the references are about the typeface with the same name. So you have cornered the market and it is a brilliant clever group of concepts. Go you! 
PS I am a broke welfare recipient so won't become a member but I am happy to watch the free videos and will always pay with a 'like'.

author: @your_utube
text: Robert I think that some of the brilliant ideas that change the world can be a cursory mention in passing. You have touched on something that I have seen happen in a way on another YT channel. Traversy Media has embarked on something like this. Brad's health is giving him troubles and he has a decent-sized channel, so rather than let it all collapse, he has decided to allow other content-providers that are less-known to present software dev videos on his channel. This gives him a much needed break as he tends to his health problems, but also grows the channel by providing more content and of a more varied nature than he is himself capable of delivering (paraphrase of his own words). In your case you can keep producing videos as you always have, but intersperse them as a regular segment or as needed basis.

replies.author: @brentverrill
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering, found it! 507  Mechanical Movements Mechanisms and Devices by Henry T. Brown, 2005 republication of nineteenth edition from 1901. First edition published in 1868.
